surfaces.surface: UF Catalog CourseLeaf (catalog.ufl.edu) — full Ribbit + program/course/sitemap API surface
surfaces.summary: Mapped the entire CourseLeaf (Leepfrog) API for the 2026 catalog. The Ribbit CGI gateway lives at /ribbit/index.cgi and serves XML-with-CDATA fragments. Three live ?page=*.rjs endpoints confirmed: getcourse.rjs (course description + Attributes/gen-ed flags + prereq edges as <a> links — VERIFIED LIVE), getcourseeco.rjs (a REVERSE INDEX: course code -> every degree program that requires it, each as a fully-qualified /UGRD/colleges-schools/<COLLEGE>/<PROGRAM>/ URL — major new discovery, builds the course->program graph for free), and getprogram.rjs (live <programinfo> XML envelope but UF does not populate it via page-code params; program data is fully server-rendered into the HTML pages instead). getcourselist.rjs / getplan.rjs / getsection.rjs / getterm.rjs exist as routes but return empty/0-bytes (not wired by UF — sections/terms come from ONE.UF, not CourseLeaf). CRITICAL FORMAT: getcourse.rjs requires the SPACE form code=COP%203530 (i.e. 'COP 3530'); the no-space form COP3502 returns an empty <courseinfo/> envelope — this is the #1 gotcha. Bulk extraction is best done off the rendered HTML, not the rjs endpoints: each /UGRD/courses/<subject>/ subject page (138 of them) contains every course as a .courseblock with title, credits, Grading Scheme, description, Prerequisite (with prereq courses as <a href='/search/?P=...' class='bubblelink code' onclick='showCourse(...)'> — prereq EDGES extractable directly), and Attributes. Each /UGRD/colleges-schools/<COLLEGE>/<PROGRAM>/ page (568 of them across 14 college codes) contains both an sc_plangrid table (semester-by-semester plan of study with credits + Critical Tracking/Quest/State Core annotations inline) and an sc_courselist table (formal requirements with 'or' alternatives, credit columns, area headers). The master crawl seed is /sitemap.xml: 785 URLs total (568 program pages, 138 subject course pages) each with a lastmod date for incremental sync. /robots.txt disallows /cim/, /courseadmin/, /azindex/ (the CIM admin/proposal system — do not crawl). No auth required on any of these; all public, all returned data this run.
surfaces.how_to_use: SDK recipe for Gradvisr. (1) DISCOVERY: GET https://catalog.ufl.edu/sitemap.xml -> parse <loc>+<lastmod>; partition into program pages (/UGRD/colleges-schools/) and subject course pages (/UGRD/courses/). Use lastmod to drive incremental re-sync. (2) COURSES + DESCRIPTIONS + PREREQ EDGES + GEN-ED FLAGS (bulk, preferred): for each of the 138 subject pages GET /UGRD/courses/<subject>/, parse every div.courseblock — p.courseblocktitle (code+title+span.credits), p.courseblockdesc (description), p.courseblockextra containing 'Grading Scheme'/'Prerequisite'/'Attributes'. Prereq edges = every <a class='bubblelink code'> inside the Prerequisite paragraph (the onclick showCourse(this,'DEPT NNNN') gives the canonical SPACE-form target code); also parse the surrounding boolean text ('(A or B) and C, minimum grade of C') for AND/OR logic and grade mins. Attributes text carries gen-ed/Quest/writing flags (e.g. 'General Education - Social Science, Satisfies 2000 Words of Writing Requirement'). (3) SINGLE-COURSE LOOKUP (on-demand, e.g. a course not yet ingested): GET /ribbit/index.cgi?page=getcourse.rjs&code=<DEPT>%20<NNNN> with the SPACE-form code -> XML <course code='..'><![CDATA[ <div class=courseblock>...]]>, same fields as above. Optionally &edition=<editioncode>. (4) COURSE->PROGRAM REVERSE GRAPH: GET /ribbit/index.cgi?page=getcourseeco.rjs&code=<DEPT>%20<NNNN> -> CDATA list of <a href='/UGRD/colleges-schools/<COLLEGE>/<PROGRAM>/index.html?find=...'> for every program referencing that course. Use this to answer 'which degrees use COP 3530' and to enumerate program pages a course matters to. (5) DEGREE REQUIREMENTS: for each program page GET /UGRD/colleges-schools/<COLLEGE>/<PROGRAM>/, extract table.sc_plangrid (ordered semester plan: codecol/titlecol/hourscol + comment rows for Quest/Critical-Tracking/State-Core) and table.sc_courselist (formal requirement groups: areaheader rows, codecol with <a class=bubblelink code> course links, hourscol credits, 'or'/comment alternative rows, listsum totals). College codes: UGACT, UGAGL, UGART, UGBUS, UGCMN, UGDCP, UGEDU, UGENG, UGHHU, UGJRC, UGLAS, UGNTR, UGNUR, UGPBH. Program slug convention = <MAJOR>_<DEGREE> (CPS_BSCS, ARO_BSAE), with nested sub-plans/minors/tracks (_UMN minor, _UCT cert/track, _UFO UF-Online, e.g. CPS_BSCS/CPS_BSCS01/). Rate: one request per page, honor lastmod for re-sync, never touch /cim/ or /courseadmin/.

endpoints.gradvisr_use: Primary single-course fetch: description, credits, grading scheme, prerequisite EDGES (as course-code links + boolean/grade-min text), and gen-ed/Quest/writing-requirement Attributes.

endpoints.name: Ribbit getprogram.rjs (program info envelope)

endpoints.returns: XML: <programinfo></programinfo> — live route but EMPTY for all page-visible program codes tried; UF does not populate this endpoint (program data is server-rendered into the HTML page instead). Likely keyed to an internal Leepfrog program ID not exposed publicly.

endpoints.gradvisr_use: None — do not use. Section/term data comes from the ONE.UF Schedule-of-Courses API, not from CourseLeaf.

endpoints.name: Course catalog sitemap (master crawl seed)
endpoints.params: none
endpoints.returns: XML urlset: 785 <url> entries, each <loc>+<lastmod>. 568 program pages (/UGRD/colleges-schools/), 138 subject course pages (/UGRD/courses/), plus advising pages. lastmod (e.g. 2026-04-11) drives incremental sync.
endpoints.gradvisr_use: The canonical enumeration of every catalog page. Partition into program vs subject URLs to drive the full ingest, and diff lastmod dates to only re-fetch changed pages.

One request returns the FULL subject.

endpoints.name: CourseLeaf JS bundle (API discovery source)
endpoints.params: optional ?v=<cachebuster>
endpoints.returns: JS source. Defines ribbiturl='/ribbit/index.cgi', showCourse()->page=getcourse.rjs&code=encodeURIComponent(which) (which = 'DEPT NNNN' space form), showCourseEco()->page=getcourseeco.rjs&code=...&prod=true. Confirms the exact request construction.
endpoints.gradvisr_use: Reference only — documents how the catalog builds Ribbit calls (the SPACE-form code requirement comes from here). Not a data endpoint.

new_discoveries: getcourseeco.rjs is a course->program REVERSE INDEX never touched by the 2022 uf_api repo: given a course code it returns every degree program referencing it, each as a fully-qualified /UGRD/colleges-schools/<COLLEGE>/<PROGRAM>/index.html?find=CODE URL — instant course-to-degree graph.

getcourse.rjs requires the SPACE form code (COP%203530); the no-space form (COP3502) silently returns an empty <courseinfo/> envelope. This is the #1 integration gotcha and likely why naive callers think the endpoint is dead.

getcourse.rjs Attributes field carries gen-ed / Quest / writing-requirement flags verbatim (e.g. 'General Education - Social Science, Satisfies 2000 Words of Writing Requirement') — directly usable for Gradvisr's gen-ed tagging.
/sitemap.xml is a complete 785-URL master index (568 program pages + 138 subject course pages) with per-page lastmod dates — the canonical crawl seed and incremental-sync driver.

Each /UGRD/courses/<subject>/ subject page returns ALL courses for that subject in one request with prereq edges already encoded as <a class='bubblelink code' onclick=showCourse(this,'DEPT NNNN')> — bulk prereq-graph extraction needs only 138 requests, no per-course calls.

Program pages carry TWO complementary requirement tables: sc_plangrid (ordered semester plan with Critical-Tracking/Quest/State-Core annotations inline) and sc_courselist (formal requirement groups with or-alternatives, credits, area headers) — both needed for a complete degree model.

14 college codes enumerated (UGACT UGAGL UGART UGBUS UGCMN UGDCP UGEDU UGENG UGHHU UGJRC UGLAS UGNTR UGNUR UGPBH); program slug convention is <MAJOR>_<DEGREE> with nested sub-plans/tracks/minors (_UMN minor, _UCT track/cert, _UFO UF-Online).

getprogram.rjs route is live but returns an empty <programinfo/> for all page-visible program codes — UF doesn't populate it; getcourselist/getplan/getsection/getterm.rjs return 0 bytes (unwired). Sections/terms are NOT in CourseLeaf — they live in ONE.UF.
robots.txt disallows the CIM admin/proposal surfaces (/cim/, /courseadmin/, /azindex/, /catalogcontents/) — out of bounds, no value for Gradvisr anyway.
recommendations: Build the SDK ingest in 3 passes off sitemap.xml: (1) fetch sitemap -> partition program/subject URLs + cache lastmod; (2) fetch 138 subject pages -> parse courseblocks into courses{code,title,credits,grading,description,attributes(gen-ed/Quest/writing)} + prereq edges; (3) fetch 568 program pages -> parse sc_courselist (requirement groups) + sc_plangrid (semester plan).

Hardcode the SPACE-form code rule for any getcourse.rjs/getcourseeco.rjs call (insert a space between dept letters and number, then URL-encode to %20). Add a unit test asserting COP3530 (no space) is normalized to 'COP 3530'.
Use getcourseeco.rjs to construct the course->program edge set and to validate/cross-check which programs require a given course — it's the cleanest reverse-graph source UF exposes.

Parse the Prerequisite paragraph's boolean text, not just the <a> links: capture AND/OR grouping, 'minimum grade of C', and non-linked codes (some prereqs like 'COP 3504' appear as plain text, not links). The onclick showCourse(this,'CODE') value is the reliable canonical target code when present.

Do NOT call getprogram.rjs / getcourselist.rjs / getsection.rjs / getterm.rjs — they're dead on UF's install. Pull sections/terms from the ONE.UF apix/soc surface (already mapped) and join on course code.
Drive incremental re-sync from sitemap lastmod diffs to stay polite (avoid re-fetching 700+ pages every run) and never touch /cim/ or /courseadmin/ per robots.txt.
Treat /search/?P= as a fallback/HTML resolver for the prereq-link hrefs; prefer getcourse.rjs for clean programmatic single-course pulls.

surfaces.summary: Introspection is ENABLED (not disabled) — full __schema/__type queries return live, this run, against https://www.ratemyprofessors.com/graphql. Single POST endpoint, Relay-style GraphQL. queryType exposes 6 root fields: newSearch, node, oauthURL, spammedTeachers, teacherTags, user. The two surfaces Gradvisr needs are newSearch (search teachers/schools within UF) and node(id) (fetch any Teacher/School/Rating by global Relay ID). AUTH: the well-known header Authorization: Basic dGVzdDp0ZXN0 (base64 of test:test, a hardcoded public client constant baked into RMP's own frontend bundle) is what every public guide uses — BUT I verified this run that data queries (node, __typename) succeed even WITHOUT the header (HTTP 200, real data). It is effectively a fully public, unauthenticated endpoint; send the header anyway to match the official client and avoid future WAF surprises. UF main campus = School id U2Nob29sLTExMDA= (legacyId 1100, Gainesville FL). Teacher node carries everything Gradvisr wants: avgRatingRounded, avgDifficultyRounded, wouldTakeAgainPercentRounded, numRatings, ratingsDistribution{r1..r5,total}, teacherRatingTags{tagName,tagCount}, courseCodes{courseName,courseCount} (real UF course codes like JPT3500, IDS2935), relatedTeachers/relatedFullTeachers, school, and the paginated ratings connection. Each Rating carries comment, date, class (course code), qualityRating, clarityRatingRounded, difficultyRatingRounded, helpfulRatingRounded, grade (student-reported A/A-/etc.), iWouldTakeAgain, isForCredit, isForOnlineClass, attendanceMandatory, textbookIsUsed, ratingTags, thumbsUp/DownTotal. ratings(first,after,courseFilter) pages via Relay cursors and can be filtered to a single course code. School node carries avgRatingRounded, numRatings, summary (10 campus-quality sub-scores, NOT academic), and departments[] (id+name, ~50 depts, a clean department taxonomy). Rate-limit posture: no per-IP throttling observed at this volume; ~10 queries this run, zero 429s. Stay polite (low concurrency, cache by teacher legacyId) — RMP has historically tolerated this client but reserves the right to block abusive IPs.
surfaces.how_to_use:
All calls are POST https://www.ratemyprofessors.com/graphql with headers: Content-Type: application/json and Authorization: Basic dGVzdDp0ZXN0 (optional but recommended — it is base64('test:test'), a public constant; omit it and queries still work). Body is {"query": "...", "variables": {...}}.

RECIPE 1 — Find UF school ID (one-time, hardcode result): query SchoolSearch($q: SchoolSearchQuery!){ newSearch{ schools(query:$q){ edges{ node{ id legacyId name city state } } } } } with variables {"q":{"text":"University of Florida"}}. Returns id "U2Nob29sLTExMDA=" / legacyId 1100. Hardcode this; never re-query.

RECIPE 2 — Search a UF professor by name: query TeacherSearch($q: TeacherSearchQuery!){ newSearch{ teachers(query:$q, first:5){ edges{ cursor node{ id legacyId firstName lastName avgRatingRounded avgDifficultyRounded numRatings wouldTakeAgainPercentRounded department school{ id name } } } pageInfo{ endCursor hasNextPage } } } } with variables {"q":{"text":"Smith","schoolID":"U2Nob29sLTExMDA="}}. TeacherSearchQuery input fields: text, schoolID, departmentID, sort, fallback. ALWAYS pass schoolID to scope to UF. Page with first + after:endCursor.

RECIPE 3 — Full teacher detail + first page of reviews (one round trip): query Teacher($id: ID!){ node(id:$id){ ... on Teacher{ id legacyId firstName lastName department avgRatingRounded avgDifficultyRounded numRatings wouldTakeAgainPercentRounded wouldTakeAgainCount ratingsDistribution{ r1 r2 r3 r4 r5 total } teacherRatingTags{ tagName tagCount } courseCodes{ courseName courseCount } ratings(first:20){ pageInfo{ endCursor hasNextPage } edges{ cursor node{ id legacyId comment date class qualityRating clarityRatingRounded difficultyRatingRounded helpfulRatingRounded grade iWouldTakeAgain isForCredit isForOnlineClass attendanceMandatory textbookIsUsed ratingTags thumbsUpTotal thumbsDownTotal } } } } } } with variables {"id":"VGVhY2hlci0yNDEwODcz"}. The node id is the opaque Relay ID returned by search (base64 of 'Teacher-<legacyId>'); you can also reconstruct it as base64('Teacher-'+legacyId).

RECIPE 4 — Page all reviews for one professor, optionally for one course: query Reviews($id: ID!, $c: String){ node(id:$id){ ... on Teacher{ ratings(first:20, after:$c, courseFilter:"JPT3500"){ pageInfo{ endCursor hasNextPage } edges{ cursor node{ comment date class qualityRating difficultyRatingRounded grade } } } } } }. Loop: pass pageInfo.endCursor as the next $c until hasNextPage=false. courseFilter is a UF course code string and is OPTIONAL (drop it for all reviews). VERIFIED: cursor paging + courseFilter both return live data.

RECIPE 5 — UF school quality + department taxonomy: query School($id: ID!){ node(id:$id){ ... on School{ id legacyId name city state numRatings avgRatingRounded summary{ schoolReputation schoolSafety careerOpportunities clubAndEventActivities foodQuality internetSpeed libraryCondition campusCondition campusLocation socialActivities schoolSatisfaction } departments{ id name } } } }.

NOTE on grade field: the Rating.grade is student-self-reported (e.g. "A", "A-", "Rather not say") and is NOT an official grade-distribution source — keep OIPR grades as the authoritative grade signal and use RMP grade only as soft corroboration. Same for courseCodes — they reflect what raters typed, so normalize/validate against the SOC catalog before trusting them as a definitive teaching history.

endpoints.name: graphql.__schema introspection
endpoints.params: body: {query:"query{__schema{queryType{fields{name}}}}"}; introspection is ENABLED
endpoints.returns: queryType fields: newSearch, node, oauthURL, spammedTeachers, teacherTags, user. __type(name) returns full field/inputField maps.
endpoints.gradvisr_use: Schema discovery — lets the SDK auto-validate query shape and detect when RMP changes fields. Not a runtime data source.

endpoints.name: newSearch.schools (find UF school ID)
endpoints.params: query SchoolSearch($q: SchoolSearchQuery!){newSearch{schools(query:$q,first:N){edges{node{id legacyId name city state}}}}}; vars {q:{text:"University of Florida"}}
endpoints.returns: SchoolSearchConnection edges -> School{id,legacyId,name,city,state}. UF main = id U2Nob29sLTExMDA=, legacyId 1100.
endpoints.gradvisr_use: One-time lookup to hardcode UF school id (U2Nob29sLTExMDA=). All teacher searches scope to it.
endpoints.method_note: POST application/json; Authorization Basic dGVzdDp0ZXN0 optional

endpoints.name: newSearch.teachers (search UF prof by name)
endpoints.params: query($q:TeacherSearchQuery!){newSearch{teachers(query:$q,first:N,after:CURSOR){edges{cursor node{...}} pageInfo{endCursor hasNextPage}}}}; TeacherSearchQuery inputFields: text, schoolID, departmentID, sort, fallback
endpoints.returns: TeacherSearchConnection edges -> Teacher node (id, legacyId, firstName, lastName, avgRatingRounded, avgDifficultyRounded, numRatings, wouldTakeAgainPercentRounded, department, school{id,name}); Relay paging via pageInfo.endCursor.
endpoints.gradvisr_use: Primary lookup: map an instructor name (from SOC sections) to an RMP Teacher node id within UF. Always pass schoolID=U2Nob29sLTExMDA=.

endpoints.name: node(id) on Teacher (full detail)
endpoints.params: query($id:ID!){node(id:$id){... on Teacher{...}}}; id = base64('Teacher-'+legacyId) or the search-returned Relay id
endpoints.returns: Teacher fields: avgDifficultyRounded, avgRatingRounded, courseCodes[]{courseName,courseCount}, department, departmentId, firstName, lastName, id, legacyId, numRatings, numNotes, ratings(connection), ratingsDistribution{r1..r5,total}, relatedTeachers, relatedFullTeachers(connection), school, teacherRatingTags[]{tagName,tagCount}, wouldTakeAgainCount, wouldTakeAgainPercentRounded, mostUsefulRating, mandatoryAttendance, takenForCredit, isSaved, lockStatus.
endpoints.gradvisr_use: The instructor-quality record: overall quality/difficulty/would-take-again, star distribution, top tags, and which UF courses this prof has taught (courseCodes). Feeds difficulty/quality scores into the planner per section.

endpoints.name: Teacher.ratings (paginated reviews)
endpoints.params: node(id:$id){... on Teacher{ratings(first:N, after:CURSOR, courseFilter:"JPT3500"){pageInfo{endCursor hasNextPage} edges{cursor node{...}}}}}; courseFilter optional
endpoints.returns: RatingConnection edges -> Rating{id,legacyId,comment,date,class(courseCode),qualityRating,clarityRatingRounded,difficultyRatingRounded,helpfulRatingRounded,grade,iWouldTakeAgain,isForCredit,isForOnlineClass,attendanceMandatory,textbookIsUsed,ratingTags,thumbsUpTotal,thumbsDownTotal,courseType,flagStatus,teacherNote}. Page via pageInfo.endCursor; courseFilter narrows to one course. VERIFIED: both after-cursor and courseFilter return live data.
endpoints.sample_response: {"node":{"class":"JPN4956","comment":"Maybe one of the best classes I've taken at UF?","date":"2025-12-28 21:43:54 +0000 UTC","qualityRating":5,"clarityRatingRounded":5,"difficultyRatingRounded":2,"helpfulRatingRounded":5,"grade":"A","isForCredit":true,"isForOnlineClass":false,"attendanceMandatory":"mandatory","textbookIsUsed":false,"ratingTags":"Get ready to read--Hilarious--Respected"}}
endpoints.gradvisr_use: Per-course student reviews with text, difficulty, helpfulness, self-reported grade, and tags. courseFilter pulls only reviews for the specific course a student is planning. ratingTags use '--' as delimiter.

endpoints.name: node(id) on School (UF quality + departments)
endpoints.params: query($id:ID!){node(id:$id){... on School{...}}}; id=U2Nob29sLTExMDA=
endpoints.returns: School{id,legacyId,name,city,state,country,numRatings,avgRatingRounded,isVisible,summary{schoolReputation,schoolSafety,careerOpportunities,clubAndEventActivities,foodQuality,internetSpeed,libraryCondition,campusCondition,campusLocation,socialActivities,schoolSatisfaction},departments[]{id,name},ratings(CampusRatingConnection)}. summary sub-scores are campus-quality (NOT academic).
endpoints.gradvisr_use: departments[] gives a clean UF department taxonomy (id+name) usable to scope teacher searches via departmentID. summary is campus-life flavor (low planner value). avgRatingRounded/numRatings are school-wide context.

endpoints.name: Teacher.relatedFullTeachers / relatedTeachers
endpoints.params: node(id:$id){... on Teacher{relatedTeachers{id firstName lastName avgRating} relatedFullTeachers(first:N){edges{node{firstName lastName avgRatingRounded numRatings}}}}}
endpoints.returns: relatedTeachers: lightweight list with avgRating (non-rounded). relatedFullTeachers: TeacherConnection with full Teacher nodes (numRatings often 0 in this projection). Both live.
endpoints.gradvisr_use: Suggest alternative instructors teaching similar/same UF courses (same department) — useful for 'who else teaches this' recommendations when a prof is full or poorly rated.

endpoints.name: teacherTags (global tag catalog)
endpoints.params: query{teacherTags{id legacyId tagName}}; no args
endpoints.returns: Global list of TeacherRatingTags definitions (the ~20 canonical RMP tags like 'Tough grader', 'Get ready to read', 'Caring', etc.). Returned empty array in this run's projection — schema-confirmed root field, data may require specific selection or be empty server-side.
endpoints.gradvisr_use: Reference dictionary of all possible tag names to normalize/parse the '--'-delimited ratingTags string on each Rating. Per-teacher teacherRatingTags{tagName,tagCount} is the live, populated source — prefer that.
endpoints.method_note: POST application/json; returned empty array this run

endpoints.name: spammedTeachers / user / oauthURL (root queries)
endpoints.params: spammedTeachers(startTime!,endTime!,minRemoved!:Int); user (current session); oauthURL(provider!)
endpoints.returns: Admin/auth-oriented root fields. spammedTeachers = moderation tooling. user = logged-in session (null when public). oauthURL = SSO redirect builder.
endpoints.sample_response: (not exercised — out of scope; user is null unauthenticated)
endpoints.gradvisr_use: None — these are account/moderation surfaces, not course/instructor data. Listed for completeness of the queryType.

new_discoveries: Introspection is ENABLED on RMP's production GraphQL (most public guides assume it's off and rely on hardcoded queries) — __schema and __type(name) both return full field/inputField/arg maps live. The SDK can self-discover and self-validate the schema.

The Authorization: Basic dGVzdDp0ZXN0 header is NOT actually required for data — node() and __typename returned HTTP 200 with real data WITHOUT it this run. It is base64('test:test'), a vestigial public client constant. Send it to mimic the official client, but the endpoint is effectively fully public/unauthenticated.

Teacher.ratings supports a courseFilter:String argument (a UF course code) that the 2022 uf_api repo era tooling rarely used — lets Gradvisr pull ONLY the reviews for the exact course a student is planning, instead of all reviews then filtering client-side.

Teacher.courseCodes{courseName,courseCount} exposes the real UF course codes a professor has been rated for (JPT3500, IDS2935, JPN4956...) with frequency counts — a free teaching-history signal to map instructors to courses without scraping SOC.
ratingsDistribution{r1,r2,r3,r4,r5,total} gives the full 1-5 star histogram per professor (not just the average) — enables a confidence/variance signal in the planner instead of a single avg.
School.departments[] returns a clean ~50-entry UF department taxonomy with stable integer ids (Accounting=1, Computer Science=11) usable as departmentID to scope teacher searches.

relatedTeachers vs relatedFullTeachers are TWO distinct fields: relatedTeachers = light list with non-rounded avgRating; relatedFullTeachers = a TeacherConnection with full nodes. Both live — good for 'who else teaches this' suggestions.

Rating node carries grade (student self-reported letter grade), iWouldTakeAgain, isForCredit, isForOnlineClass, attendanceMandatory, textbookIsUsed, and ratingTags ('--'-delimited) — richer per-review structure than the legacy /paginate/professors/ratings REST endpoint the old repo used.
UF stable IDs to hardcode: School id=U2Nob29sLTExMDA= legacyId=1100. Teacher Relay id = base64('Teacher-'+legacyId), so legacyId<->id is reconstructable without a round trip.
Rate-limit posture: ~10 queries this run, zero 429s, no special headers needed beyond Content-Type. No evidence of per-IP throttling at low volume.
recommendations: Hardcode UF school id U2Nob29sLTExMDA= (legacyId 1100) and the Authorization: Basic dGVzdDp0ZXN0 header as constants in the SDK; never re-query the school. Reconstruct Teacher Relay ids as base64('Teacher-'+legacyId) to skip lookups.

Match instructors by name via newSearch.teachers with schoolID always set to UF. Cache the resulting legacyId->Teacher mapping keyed on normalized instructor name from the SOC sections; RMP names are messy (trailing spaces, e.g. 'Deborah '/'Smith ') so normalize/trim and dedupe.
For per-course quality, use Teacher.ratings(courseFilter:<SOC course code>) so the planner shows reviews/difficulty for the exact course being planned, not the professor's global average.

Treat Rating.grade and Teacher.courseCodes as SOFT signals only — grades are student-self-reported and course codes reflect rater free-text. Keep OIPR grade distributions as the authoritative grade source and validate course codes against the SOC catalog before display.
Use ratingsDistribution + numRatings to compute a confidence/variance score; suppress or caveat ratings where numRatings is very low (e.g. <5) to avoid misleading single-review averages.

Be a polite client: low concurrency, cache aggressively by legacyId, dedupe in-flight requests, exponential backoff on any 429/5xx. Do not loop-scrape all UF professors — fetch on demand per planned section.

Do not rely on teacherTags (global) for tag data — it returned empty; use per-teacher teacherRatingTags{tagName,tagCount} which is live and populated, and parse Rating.ratingTags by splitting on '--'.

Because introspection is live, add a CI canary that re-runs __type(name:'Teacher') and __type(name:'Rating') and diffs the field list — RMP can rename/remove fields without notice, and this catches breakage before it hits students.

surfaces.surface: UF Directory (directory.ufl.edu / phonebook.ufl.edu) + GatorEvals official course-evaluation public results (gatorevals.aa.ufl.edu)
surfaces.summary: Two distinct surfaces. (1) UF DIRECTORY is a server-rendered Java/JSP app (jsessionid in URLs), NOT a JSON API. Public people search = POST (also accepts GET-with-params but GET without POST returns the empty form) to /directory/SearchPerson with firstName/lastName/email/auth. auth=false returns Faculty+Staff with NO login (verified live: returned Dobson records with name, phone, email, dept). auth=true requires GatorLink SSO (adds students) — out of scope. Results are HTML 'list-card' blocks (name, phone, email, opaque detail link). Following the detail link (/directory/indv/<token>/<idx>) yields name + affiliation type ('Staff'/'Faculty') + Department (e.g. 'RE-HUB') + email + phone. This is a clean SOC-instructor-name -> {full name, department, ufl.edu email, faculty/staff flag} resolver. phonebook.ufl.edu 301-redirects to directory.ufl.edu. (2) GATOREVALS public-results page is NOT a custom API — it is a Tableau Public embed (object class='tableauViz', viz_v1.js loader) of workbook 'GatorEvalsTableauPublic3TermstoFall2025'. Verified live: the Tableau Public profile metadata API (public.tableau.com/profile/api/workbook/<name>) returns JSON exposing TWO sheets — 'GatorEvalsPublicData' (raw evaluation data table) and 'GatorEvalsPublicDashboard' (the viz) — author john.j6434, 8209 views, lastUpdate ~2026-02. The underlying eval data IS extractable but ONLY via the Tableau bootstrapSession protocol (load embed -> JS mints sessionid -> POST /vizql/w/<wb>/v/<view>/bootstrapSession -> parse data segments), or with the open-source `tableauscraper` Python lib. Direct .csv export is blocked (302/404). No simple param-driven JSON endpoint (institution/term/course/instructor) exists — the dashboard's filtering is internal Tableau state. So GatorEvals = official UF eval scores, machine-reachable via Tableau scraping, complementary to RMP but heavier to ingest.
surfaces.how_to_use: DIRECTORY instructor resolver (core for Gradvisr): To resolve a SOC instructor name to dept/email, POST https://directory.ufl.edu/directory/SearchPerson with form body `firstName=&lastName=<LAST>&email=&auth=false` (Content-Type application/x-www-form-urlencoded), UA Mozilla/5.0. Parse HTML for `<div class="list-card">` blocks: name is in the gray-box anchor ('Last, First M'), phone + email are labeled anchors, and the detail href `/directory/indv/<token>/<n>` (strip the `;jsessionid=...`). For department, GET https://directory.ufl.edu/directory/indv/<token>/<n> and parse the `heading-black-titles` h1 ('Name, Affiliation') + the `Department:` label's sibling value. Match SOC instructor names (which are 'Last, First' style) directly. Keep to a handful of requests; this is a JSP app, not a bulk API — cache results. GATOREVALS eval scores (enrichment): Step 1 GET https://public.tableau.com/profile/api/workbook/GatorEvalsTableauPublic3TermstoFall2025 for the JSON sheet list + last-update timestamp (cheap freshness check). Step 2 to pull actual scores, run a Tableau-Public bootstrap: load https://public.tableau.com/views/GatorEvalsTableauPublic3TermstoFall2025/GatorEvalsPublicData?:embed=y&:showVizHome=no, capture the sessionid the JS creates, POST to /vizql/w/GatorEvalsTableauPublic3TermstoFall2025/v/GatorEvalsPublicData/bootstrapSession/sessions/<id>, and parse the returned dataDictionary/worksheet panes — easiest via the `tableauscraper` pip package (TableauScraper().loads(url).getWorksheet('GatorEvals Public Data').data -> a pandas frame of institution/term/course/instructor + eval metrics). This is a one-time-per-term batch ingest job (the workbook is '3 terms to Fall 2025'), NOT a live per-query call.

endpoints.name: Directory People Search (Faculty/Staff, public)
endpoints.params: firstName, lastName, email, auth (false=Faculty+Staff no-login; true=adds Students, requires GatorLink SSO)
endpoints.returns: text/html. Search-results page with N `<div class="list-card">` blocks. Each card: display name 'Last, First M' (gray-box anchor), Phone (labeled anchor + tel:), Email (labeled anchor + mailto:, the ufl.edu address), and a detail link /directory/indv/<base64ish-token>/<index>;jsessionid=<...>.
endpoints.sample_response: list-card -> 'Dobson, Jon P' | Phone 352-392-0911 | Email jdobson@ufl.edu | href /directory/indv/LTpEb2Jzb246LTot/0 . Returned ~6 Dobson Faculty/Staff records live.
endpoints.gradvisr_use: Resolve SOC instructor names -> official ufl.edu email + faculty/staff flag; the canonical UF identity for cross-referencing RMP and GatorEvals.

endpoints.name: Directory Individual Detail (dept/title/affiliation)
endpoints.params: token + index come from a SearchPerson result href; jsessionid optional (works without it)
endpoints.returns: text/html. h1.heading-black-titles = 'Name, Affiliation' (Affiliation = 'Staff' or 'Faculty'); a 'Staff Information:' / 'Faculty Information:' section with a `Department:` label whose value is the unit code/name (e.g. 'RE-HUB'); email + phone repeated.
endpoints.sample_response: h1 'Jon P. Dobson, Staff'; Department: RE-HUB; jdobson@ufl.edu; 352-392-0911
endpoints.gradvisr_use: Get an instructor's home department + faculty-vs-staff classification to disambiguate which 'J. Smith' teaches a given SOC course and tag department-of-record.

endpoints.name: Directory People Search (auth=true, full incl. students)
endpoints.params: firstName, lastName, email, auth=true
endpoints.returns: Redirects to GatorLink SSO (login.ufl.edu / Shibboleth). Requires authentication.
endpoints.sample_response: SSO wall — not retrieved (out of scope, do not evade).
endpoints.gradvisr_use: Do NOT use — student data behind SSO; auth=false already covers all instructors (faculty/staff).

endpoints.name: phonebook.ufl.edu (legacy host)
endpoints.params: none
endpoints.returns: HTTP 200 after 301 redirect to https://directory.ufl.edu/ — same app.
endpoints.sample_response: FINAL url = https://directory.ufl.edu/ (size 34148, the directory home/form).
endpoints.gradvisr_use: Legacy alias — always hit directory.ufl.edu directly; phonebook just redirects.

endpoints.name: GatorEvals Public Results page (Tableau embed shell)
endpoints.params: none
endpoints.returns: text/html (Joomla/uf2015 template). Body embeds a Tableau Public viz: object.tableauViz with param name='name' value='GatorEvalsTableauPublic3TermstoFall2025/GatorEvalsPublicDashboard', host_url=public.tableau.com, loaded via https://public.tableau.com/javascripts/api/viz_v1.js. No JSON API of its own; a secondary search box posts to search.ufl.edu (Google CSE), unrelated to evals.
endpoints.gradvisr_use: Discovery anchor — tells you the exact Tableau Public workbook holding official UF eval scores; you then scrape Tableau, not this page.

endpoints.name: Tableau Public Workbook Metadata API (GatorEvals)
endpoints.params: workbook name in path
endpoints.returns: application/json. Keys: defaultViewName, authorName, profileName, viewCount, lastUpdateDate (epoch ms), title, description, viewInfos[] (each {sheetRepoUrl, sheetTitle}).
endpoints.gradvisr_use: Cheap freshness/version check (lastUpdateDate) and confirms the hidden raw-data sheet 'GatorEvals Public Data' to target when scraping eval scores per term.

endpoints.name: GatorEvals raw data sheet (Tableau bootstrapSession)
endpoints.params: :embed=y, :showVizHome=no; session id minted client-side; bootstrap POST body carries sheet/filter state
endpoints.returns: Tableau bootstrap payload (concatenated JSON segments) containing the worksheet data table: institution/term/course/instructor + evaluation metric columns. Extractable with `tableauscraper` (TS().loads(url).getWorksheet('GatorEvals Public Data').data). Plain .csv export blocked (302/404).
endpoints.sample_response: Not fully materialized this run (requires JS-minted sessionid / tableauscraper). Confirmed reachable: view page loads (200), tsConfig vizqlPrefix='vizql', isPublic=true; sheet 'GatorEvalsPublicData' confirmed via metadata API.
endpoints.gradvisr_use: OFFICIAL UF course-evaluation scores per course/instructor/term — the institutional complement to crowd-sourced RMP. Batch-ingest once per term via tableauscraper; map onto SOC instructor names resolved through the Directory.

endpoints.name: Tableau Public direct CSV export (GatorEvals)
endpoints.params: none
endpoints.returns: Blocked — 302 redirect (no body) / 404 JSON error. Tableau Public disables anonymous direct crosstab CSV; must go through bootstrapSession.
endpoints.gradvisr_use: Don't rely on it — use the bootstrapSession/tableauscraper path instead.

new_discoveries: UF Directory exposes a NO-LOGIN Faculty/Staff search via POST /directory/SearchPerson with auth=false — the 2022 uf_api repo never covered this; it's a clean instructor-name -> {ufl.edu email, department, faculty/staff flag} resolver.

Directory is a server-rendered JSP app (jsessionid), not JSON: GET on /SearchPerson without form POST returns the empty form (size 34148); you MUST POST (or GET with the firstName/lastName/auth params) to get list-card results.
Individual detail pages /directory/indv/<token>/<index> carry the Department field and the Faculty-vs-Staff affiliation in the h1, enabling 'J. Smith' disambiguation across SOC sections.
GatorEvals public-results is NOT a bespoke API — it's a Tableau Public embed of workbook 'GatorEvalsTableauPublic3TermstoFall2025' (author john.j6434).

Tableau Public's metadata API (public.tableau.com/profile/api/workbook/<name>) returns JSON and exposed a HIDDEN raw-data sheet 'GatorEvals Public Data' alongside the visible dashboard — that sheet is the extraction target for per-course/instructor eval scores.

Official UF eval scores ARE machine-extractable (via Tableau bootstrapSession / the open-source tableauscraper lib), giving Gradvisr an institutional difficulty/quality signal to complement RateMyProfessors — but it's a per-term batch scrape, not a live query API.
phonebook.ufl.edu is a dead alias that 301s to directory.ufl.edu.
recommendations: Build a Directory client that POSTs /directory/SearchPerson (auth=false) by lastName, then for the top match GETs /directory/indv/... to grab department + faculty/staff flag. Cache by normalized name; rate-limit to a few req/run — it's a JSP app, not a bulk API. Treat 'Last, First M' parsing as the join key against SOC instructor strings.
NEVER set auth=true — it walls to GatorLink SSO and would touch student data; auth=false already covers every instructor (faculty/staff).

For GatorEvals, ingest once per term: hit the Tableau metadata API for lastUpdateDate (skip if unchanged), then use the `tableauscraper` Python lib against the 'GatorEvals Public Data' sheet to pull the official course/instructor eval table. Persist it; do NOT call Tableau live per planner query.

Use GatorEvals (official UF instrument) as the authoritative difficulty/quality layer and RMP as the crowd layer; store both, prefer GatorEvals for credibility, fall back to RMP for coverage gaps and recent terms.
Do not attempt the blocked .csv export path; standardize on bootstrapSession/tableauscraper.
HTML parsing of the Directory is brittle (JSP markup can change) — pair with Scrapling adaptive selectors and add a contract test on the list-card/Department structure.

endpoints.name: LibCal Hours Today (open, unauthenticated)
endpoints.params: iid=6 (UF institution id, REQUIRED), lid=0 for ALL 17 locations or lid=<id> for one (e.g. 7728=Library West, 8622=Marston Science, 8625=Smathers, 8627=Education, 8871=Health Science Center, 22838=Marston Makerspace), format=json
endpoints.returns: {locations:[{lid,name,category,desc(html),url,lat,long,color,day,times:{status:'open'|'closed',hours:[{from:'10am',to:'6pm'}],currently_open:bool},rendered:'10am - 6pm'}]}
endpoints.gradvisr_use: Real-time 'is the library open right now / today' for a where-to-study layer. 17 UF library locations with today's hours, open/closed status, lat/long, contact. Zero auth, single GET.

endpoints.name: LibCal Hours Grid (multi-week)
endpoints.params: iid=6 REQUIRED, lid=0 (all) or single lid, weeks=1..N (how many weeks of grid), format=json
endpoints.returns: {locations:[{lid,name,category,url,color,weeks:[{Sunday:{date,times:{status,hours:[{from,to}],note?,currently_open}},Monday:{...},...}]}]}  — note field carries holiday labels e.g. 'Memorial Day'
endpoints.gradvisr_use: Weekly study-hours planner + holiday closure detection (note field surfaces 'Memorial Day', exam-week hours, etc). Pairs with academic-calendar for 'where can I study during finals'.

endpoints.name: LibCal Hours by Date
endpoints.params: iid=6 REQUIRED, lid=0 or single lid, date=YYYY-MM-DD, format=json
endpoints.returns: {locations:[{lid,name,date,times:{status,hours,currently_open},rendered}]}
endpoints.gradvisr_use: Hours for an arbitrary future date — 'will Marston be open the day my exam ends'. Lighter than the grid for single-date lookups.

endpoints.name: LibCal Hours Grid Widget (alias)
endpoints.params: iid=6 REQUIRED, format=json, weeks optional — identical payload to api_hours_grid.php
endpoints.returns: Same shape as api_hours_grid.php (locations[].weeks[].{day}.times)
endpoints.gradvisr_use: Backup/alias of the grid endpoint; use api_hours_grid.php as primary.

endpoints.name: LibCal Spaces Availability Grid (study-room booking grid)
endpoints.params: form-encoded: lid=<location id> gid=<space-GROUP id> start=YYYY-MM-DD end=YYYY-MM-DD; REQUIRES header 'Referer: https://libcal.uflib.ufl.edu/spaces?lid=<lid>' and 'X-Requested-With: XMLHttpRequest'. Without Referer => 403 'Invalid Referrer'. With Referer but wrong/empty gid => 400 'Invalid value' (must supply a real space-group id, discoverable from the /spaces?lid= SPA).
endpoints.returns: JSON slot grid of bookable rooms/seats (per-room time-slot availability). Shape not captured this run (gated behind valid gid).
endpoints.sample_response: (403 'Invalid Referrer' without Referer; 400 'Invalid value' with Referer + gid=0 — endpoint confirmed live, needs real gid)
endpoints.gradvisr_use: Live study-room availability for the where-to-study layer. Recipe: scrape gid from the /spaces?lid=<lid> page once per location, then POST with that Referer. Booking itself requires login; READING availability is public+Referer-gated. Treat as best-effort enrichment.

endpoints.name: LibCal Calendar Events List (ajax)
endpoints.params: Endpoint confirmed live (referenced in LibCal_public.min.js as /ajax/calendar/list/). Exact param contract not solved — c=0 alone => 400 'Invalid value'; needs the calendar's category/audience/campus filter ids (cid/aud/cat) discoverable from libcal.uflib.ufl.edu/calendar page. Public calendar feed exists at /calendar/UFLibrariesEvents.
endpoints.returns: JSON list of library events (workshops, instruction sessions, study-skills events).
endpoints.sample_response: (400 'Invalid value' with c=0 — endpoint live, param shape unresolved)
endpoints.gradvisr_use: Library workshops / research-skills events feed (low planner value). De-prioritize; the calendar page also exposes iCal/RSS feeds at /calendar/UFLibrariesEvents for simpler ingestion.

endpoints.name: LibCal Equipment Availability Nextdate
endpoints.params: Confirmed live (referenced in LibCal_public.min.js). Returns next available date for borrowable equipment (cameras, laptops, chargers). Needs a category/item id param (bare call => 400 'no future availability in this category').
endpoints.returns: JSON next-available date for equipment loans.
endpoints.sample_response: (400 'Sorry, there is no future availability in this category.' — endpoint live, needs cid)
endpoints.gradvisr_use: Equipment-loan availability. Not relevant to degree planning; skip.

endpoints.name: LibCal v1.1 REST (documented Springshare API) — OAuth WALLED
endpoints.params: Documented Springshare endpoints: /api/1.1/hours/{lid}, /api/1.1/space/locations, /api/1.1/space/categories/{lid}, /api/1.1/space/item/{id}, /api/1.1/space/bookings, /api/1.1/calendars, /api/1.1/events. ALL require OAuth2 client_credentials (client_id + client_secret => bearer token). No public token.
endpoints.returns: 403 'You do not have permission to perform this operation.' for every path without a bearer token.
endpoints.sample_response: HTTP 403 'You do not have permission to perform this operation.'
endpoints.gradvisr_use: The richer official API (full space inventory, booking objects, calendar metadata) but needs UF Libraries to issue OAuth credentials. Do NOT pursue — the open api_hours_*.php widgets cover hours, and Primo covers catalog. Only relevant if UF grants Gradvisr a LibCal API key.

endpoints.name: Primo VE Public Search (primaws/pnxs) — UNAUTH catalog discovery
endpoints.params: vid=01FALSC_UFL:UFL (UF view id, REQUIRED), tab=Everything, scope=MyInst_and_CI (institution+CDI) or MyInst_and_CI variants, q=<field>,<op>,<terms> (e.g. any,contains,calculus / title,contains,...), limit, offset, lang=en, sort optional. NO apikey and NO JWT header needed — Primo auto-issues a guest token for public discovery. host = ufl-flvc.primo.exlibrisgroup.com (UF's Primo VE search-base).
endpoints.returns: {info:{totalResultsLocal,totalResultsPC,total,first,last}, highlights, docs:[{pnx:{display:{title,creator,publisher,type,...},control:{recordid,...},addata:{},sort,facets}}], timelog}
endpoints.sample_response: {"info":{"totalResultsLocal":9825,"totalResultsPC":4937145,"total":4946970},"docs":[{"pnx":{"display":{"title":["A text-book of organic chemistry /"],"creator":["Bernthsen, A. ..."],"type":["text_resource"],"publisher":["Blackie & Son,"]}}}]}
endpoints.gradvisr_use: Live, fully-unauthenticated UF catalog discovery. For a course like CHM2210, query the textbook/topic and surface 'find this at the library' results (title/author/type/location). Full PNX bibliographic record (display/control/addata). The genuinely useful library API for a planner — no key, no scraping, real JSON.

endpoints.name: Primo VE Public Configuration REST
endpoints.params: vid=01FALSC_UFL:UFL in path. No auth.
endpoints.returns: {beaconO22, primo-view, tiles, system-configuration, mapping-tables, authentication, scopes-context-map, backend_system, customization, UIComponents, queryTerms, ...} — exposes the valid scopes, tabs, and query fields you can pass to /pnxs
endpoints.gradvisr_use: Read once to discover valid tab/scope/query-field values for the pnxs search (the scopes-context-map and queryTerms sections). Lets the SDK build correct Primo queries without guessing. No auth.

endpoints.name: Ex Libris Hosted Primo REST (api-na) — APIKEY WALLED
endpoints.params: Same query semantics as pnxs but REQUIRES an Ex Libris Developer Network apikey. Bad/missing key => UNAUTHORIZED.
endpoints.returns: XML/JSON <web_service_result><errorList><error><errorCode>UNAUTHORIZED</errorCode> when apikey invalid.
endpoints.gradvisr_use: The 'official' Primo API but requires an apikey. SKIP IT — the unauthenticated primaws/pnxs path above returns the same catalog data with no key. Documented here only to steer the SDK away from the key-walled host.

endpoints.name: LibGuides v1.2 API (lgapi-us) — APIKEY WALLED
endpoints.params: site_id=108 (UF Libraries LibGuides site id, discovered from guides.uflib.ufl.edu), key=<API key REQUIRED>, plus status, sort_by, expand (e.g. expand=owner,subjects,pages). Endpoints: /1.2/guides, /1.2/subjects, /1.2/assets, /1.2/az (databases A-Z), /1.2/accounts. Bad key => clean 401 JSON {status:401}.
endpoints.returns: 401 {"status":401,"message":null,"more_info":""} without key. With key: array of guides {id,name,description,url,owner,subjects,pages[]}.
endpoints.gradvisr_use: Subject research guides + databases-A-Z. Course-relevant ('research guide for ENC1101') but needs a UF LibGuides API key. Low planner priority — defer unless UF grants a key. site_id=108 captured for when/if a key is obtained.

endpoints.name: LibGuides Public Site (guides.uflib.ufl.edu) — scrapable HTML
endpoints.params: Public LibGuides front-end. site_id=108 embedded in page config. Individual guides at guides.uflib.ufl.edu/<slug>; A-Z databases at guides.uflib.ufl.edu/az. HTML, no JSON API without the keyed lgapi above.
endpoints.returns: HTML pages (subject guides, course guides, A-Z database list). Parse with Scrapling if needed.
endpoints.gradvisr_use: If course/subject research guides are wanted without an API key, scrape the public HTML (Scrapling, --ai-targeted). Map subject guides to majors. Enrichment only.

endpoints.name: UF Libraries main site (uflib.ufl.edu) — hours/find portal
endpoints.params: WordPress site (HTTP 200, 155KB). Exposes the Primo vid, libcal.uflib.ufl.edu links, and /find/ catalog portal. WP REST likely at /wp-json/wp/v2/ (not probed this run).
endpoints.returns: HTML; embeds primo vid=01FALSC_UFL:UFL and libcal vanity host.
endpoints.sample_response: HTTP 200; references libcal.uflib.ufl.edu/hours/, primo vid=01FALSC_UFL:UFL, ufl-flvc.primo.exlibrisgroup.com
endpoints.gradvisr_use: Discovery hub only — gave us the Primo vid and the LibCal vanity host. Not a data API itself. The WP /wp-json content REST could supply library news/blog posts but has no planner value.

surfaces.host: libcal.uflib.ufl.edu (LibCal vanity for uflib.libcal.com) + ufl-flvc.primo.exlibrisgroup.com (Primo VE) + guides.uflib.ufl.edu / lgapi-us.libapps.com (LibGuides) + uflib.ufl.edu (portal)
surfaces.how_to_use:
SANDBOX DNS NOTE: uflib.libcal.com does NOT resolve here — use UF's vanity CNAME libcal.uflib.ufl.edu for ALL LibCal calls (identical Springshare backend, iid=6). Likewise generic primo.exlibrisgroup.com fails; use ufl-flvc.primo.exlibrisgroup.com.

TWO endpoints are the keepers (open, JSON, no auth):
1) LIBRARY HOURS (campus-life layer): GET https://libcal.uflib.ufl.edu/api_hours_today.php?iid=6&lid=0&format=json (all 17 locations, today) — or api_hours_grid.php?...&weeks=N for a week grid (holiday notes included), or api_hours_date.php?...&date=YYYY-MM-DD for one day. Filter one library with &lid=<id> (7728=Library West, 8622=Marston Science, 8625=Smathers). iid=6 is mandatory.
2) CATALOG SEARCH (find-this-book layer): GET https://ufl-flvc.primo.exlibrisgroup.com/primaws/rest/pub/pnxs?vid=01FALSC_UFL:UFL&tab=Everything&scope=MyInst_and_CI&q=any,contains,<urlencoded%20terms>&limit=10&offset=0&lang=en — NO apikey, NO JWT header (Primo auto-issues a guest token). Read /primaws/rest/pub/configuration/vid/01FALSC_UFL:UFL once to get valid scope/tab/query-field values.

GATED (record, don't chase): LibCal study-room availability grid (POST /spaces/availability/grid, needs Referer + a real space-group gid scraped from /spaces?lid=<lid>) is best-effort. LibCal /api/1.1/ REST, Ex Libris api-na Primo, and LibGuides lgapi all need credentials (403/401/UNAUTHORIZED verified) — skip unless UF issues keys.

ALL library data is gradvisr_relevance=enrichment at most — a future where/when-to-study layer + a 'find course materials at the library' tie-in. NONE is core degree-planning. Build a thin LibrariesClient with two methods: getHours(lid?, date?) and searchCatalog(query) — both unauthenticated.
new_discoveries: uflib.libcal.com is unreachable in sandbox DNS but the UF vanity CNAME libcal.uflib.ufl.edu serves the IDENTICAL LibCal backend (iid=6) — use it for all LibCal calls

LibCal exposes THREE fully-open unauthenticated hours endpoints the 2022 uf_api repo never touched: api_hours_today.php, api_hours_grid.php (with weeks= and holiday 'note' fields), api_hours_date.php — all return clean JSON, iid=6 + lid filter

Parsed all 17 UF library location ids in one call (Library West=7728, Marston Science=8622, Marston Makerspace=22838, Smathers=8625, Education=8627, Health Science Center=8871, Architecture/Fine Arts=8626, plus Jacksonville/Jupiter/Scripps satellites)

Primo VE public search REST (primaws/rest/pub/pnxs) is the big find: completely unauthenticated UF catalog discovery returning full PNX bibliographic records — 9825 local hits for 'organic chemistry' — no apikey, no JWT header, host ufl-flvc.primo.exlibrisgroup.com, vid=01FALSC_UFL:UFL
Primo public configuration REST (primaws/rest/pub/configuration/vid/...) exposes valid scopes/tabs/query-fields so the SDK can build correct queries without guessing

Confirmed the credential walls precisely: LibCal /api/1.1/ => 403 (OAuth), Ex Libris api-na Primo => UNAUTHORIZED (apikey), LibGuides lgapi-us.libapps.com 1.2 => clean 401 (apikey). The open widget/primaws layers fully substitute for these for hours+catalog.
LibGuides UF site_id=108 captured; A-Z databases at guides.uflib.ufl.edu/az; public HTML scrapable if no key

LibCal study-room availability grid endpoint exists (POST /spaces/availability/grid) but is Referer-CSRF-gated AND needs a real space-group gid — reading availability is technically public but requires per-location gid discovery; booking needs login
recommendations: Ship a minimal LibrariesClient now with two unauth methods: getHours({lid?, date?, weeks?}) hitting libcal.uflib.ufl.edu/api_hours_*.php?iid=6, and searchCatalog(query, {limit,scope}) hitting ufl-flvc.primo.exlibrisgroup.com/primaws/rest/pub/pnxs?vid=01FALSC_UFL:UFL. Both zero-credential, both verified-live this run.
Hardcode the 17 location ids (and iid=6) as a constant map — they're stable; one api_hours_today.php?lid=0 call enumerates them if they ever change.
Use the UF vanity host libcal.uflib.ufl.edu, NOT uflib.libcal.com — the latter failed DNS in this environment and the vanity CNAME is the safer canonical choice anyway.

For catalog, fetch /primaws/rest/pub/configuration once at init to cache valid scope/tab/queryTerms, then build q=<field>,contains,<terms>. Map a course's known textbook/topic to a Primo search for a 'find at the library' enrichment card.

Do NOT integrate the credentialed APIs (LibCal /api/1.1/, Ex Libris api-na, LibGuides lgapi) — all verified walled and the open layers cover hours+catalog. Only revisit if UF Libraries grants Gradvisr OAuth/apikey credentials.

Treat study-room availability (spaces/availability/grid) as a v2 stretch: it needs per-location gid scraping + Referer header, gives diminishing returns vs. just linking to libcal.uflib.ufl.edu/spaces. Link out rather than proxy.
Tag every library feature enrichment in the SDK — never let it block or appear in core degree-planning paths. It's a 'where/when to study + find materials' side layer.
surfaces.summary: UF Libraries surface mapped and verified live. Two genuinely usable UNAUTHENTICATED JSON APIs the 2022 uf_api repo never covered: (1) LibCal hours via the UF vanity host libcal.uflib.ufl.edu (api_hours_today.php / api_hours_grid.php / api_hours_date.php, iid=6, all 17 location ids parsed, holiday-note aware) and (2) Primo VE public catalog search via ufl-flvc.primo.exlibrisgroup.com/primaws/rest/pub/pnxs (vid=01FALSC_UFL:UFL, no apikey, no JWT — returned 9825 real hits for 'organic chemistry' with full PNX records). The 'official' credentialed layers were all probed and confirmed walled: LibCal /api/1.1/ (403 OAuth), Ex Libris api-na Primo (UNAUTHORIZED apikey), LibGuides lgapi 1.2 (401 apikey, UF site_id=108). LibCal study-room availability grid exists but is Referer-CSRF + gid gated (best-effort v2). DNS gotcha: uflib.libcal.com and generic primo.exlibrisgroup.com don't resolve in sandbox — use the UF vanity/institution hosts. All library data rated gradvisr_relevance=enrichment: a future where/when-to-study + find-course-materials layer, never core planning. Recommend a thin two-method LibrariesClient (getHours + searchCatalog), both zero-auth.
surfaces.surface: UF Libraries (uflib) — LibCal hours/spaces, Primo VE catalog search, LibGuides

endpoints.name: CampusMap searchBldg (building + room directory w/ lat-lon)
endpoints.params: q=<string> (appears loosely filtered/ignored — returns FULL directory regardless: 4149 rows, 2789 distinct buildings). Requires browser UA; Referer https://campusmap.ufl.edu/ recommended.
endpoints.returns: JSON array of building/room records. Keys: BLDGCODE, BLDG (4-digit code), NAME, ABBREV, OFFICIAL_ROOM_NAME, LAT, LON, SITECODE, BLDG_NAME. One row per (building x official room).
endpoints.gradvisr_use: THIS is the gold for 'time between back-to-back classes'. Map each section's building code/abbrev (from ONE.UF soc) -> LAT/LON here, compute haversine/walk-time between consecutive class buildings, warn students about tight back-to-back schedules across campus. 2789 buildings geocoded.

endpoints.name: CampusMap layer assets (60+ GeoJSON FeatureCollections)
endpoints.params: No params. Path = https://campusmap.ufl.edu/assets/<FILENAME>. Filenames enumerated live from assets/sidenav.json manifest. Verified live: bus_stops, campus_dining, libraries, rec_fitness, computer_labs, printers, restrooms_unisex, ev_charging, bike_racks, blue_phones, aeds, health_wellness, snap_stops, scooter, ada_entrances, ss_registrar, ss_reitz, trails, boundaries, parking_polys. Full manifest list (60+): ada_entrances, ada_parking, ada_routes, aeds, atm_locations, bike_racks, blue_phones, boundaries, bus_stops, campus_dining, carpool_parking, closure_*, computer_labs, emergency_speakers, ev_charging, fix_it_stations, health_wellness, historic_district, historical_register, housing_*, lactation_rooms, libraries, metered_parking, move_in, printers, project_areas, rec_fitness, restrooms_family, restrooms_unisex, scooter, snap_stops, ss_bookstore, ss_cwc, ss_disability, ss_dso, ss_field_fork, ss_housing, ss_hub, ss_registrar, ss_reitz, ss_shcc, ss_taps, ss_ufpd, ss_vet, trails, waps, zip_cars. Needs browser UA; Referer https://campusmap.ufl.edu/.
endpoints.returns: GeoJSON FeatureCollection. {type:'FeatureCollection', features:[{type:'Feature', geometry:{type:'Point'|'Polygon', coordinates:[...]}, properties:{...varies per layer: stop_id/stop_name/RATING, NAME/Locations, CUSTOM_POPUP(HTML), etc.}}]}
endpoints.sample_response: bus_stops.json: {"type":"Feature","geometry":{"type":"Point","coordinates":[-82.3227,29.6456]},"properties":{"stop_id":1,"RATING":"Not ADA Evaluated","stop_name":"Rosa Parks Downtown Station"}}  |  campus_dining.json: {"NAME":"Marston Science Library","Locations":"Starbucks","CUSTOM_POPUP":"...dineoncampus.com/UF/hours-of-operation..."}
endpoints.gradvisr_use: Enrichment layer for a campus-aware planner: place student amenities (libraries, computer_labs, printers, dining, restrooms, rec_fitness, ev_charging, study spaces) near their class buildings. snap_stops + bus_stops + ada_routes support 'how do I get from class A to class B'. campus_dining links to dineoncampus.com hours. This is the relocated home of the 2022 cmapjson/*.json data.

endpoints.name: CampusMap sidenav layer manifest
endpoints.params: None. Browser UA recommended.
endpoints.returns: JSON array of layer-group categories. Each: {TITLE, ICON, PRIMARY_HEX, LAYERS:[{NAME, ICON, FILENAME}]}. FILENAME values are the asset json files above; some LAYERS point to ArcGIS VectorTileServer URLs (e.g. //tiles.arcgis.com/.../Campus_Connector_*_Route/VectorTileServer for the campus shuttle routes).
endpoints.gradvisr_use: Self-describing manifest — fetch this first to discover every available campus layer + its filename and category, then lazy-fetch the assets you want. Future-proofs the SDK against UF adding/renaming layers.

endpoints.name: CampusMap boundaries / parking_polys (large GeoJSON)
endpoints.params: None. Sibling: https://campusmap.ufl.edu/assets/parking_polys.json (1.5MB parking decal polygons).
endpoints.returns: GeoJSON FeatureCollection of campus boundary polygons (2.2MB) / parking decal zone polygons (1.5MB).
endpoints.gradvisr_use: Map-rendering polish only (draw campus footprint / parking zones). Not planner-critical. parking_polys could power a 'which decal do I need for my class building' nicety.

endpoints.name: STARS facilities public building photo
endpoints.params: bldg=<4-digit BLDG code from searchBldg> (e.g. 0001). PUBLIC path (/public/). Browser UA fine.
endpoints.returns: Binary image/jpeg of the building (verified ~1.2MB JPEG for bldg=0001). Note /public/api/ root itself returns 403 (directory listing denied) but the photo endpoint with a bldg param returns the image.
endpoints.sample_response: (binary JPEG, content-type image/jpeg, 1191459 bytes for bldg=0001 University Auditorium)
endpoints.gradvisr_use: Show a real photo of each class building in the schedule/planner UI keyed by BLDG code. Pairs with searchBldg geocoding for a rich 'your classes are here' campus view.

endpoints.name: CampusMap legacy cmapjson static files (DEAD)
endpoints.params: The 2022 uf_api targets: dining.json, study.json, bus_stops.json, geo_buildings.json, aed.json (and parking/emergency_phones/buildings). All hard-403.
endpoints.returns: Apache 403 Forbidden (text/html, 199 bytes). NOT a WAF/UA block — returns 403 even WITH realistic browser UA + Referer https://campusmap.ufl.edu/. The /library/cmapjson/ directory is deny-all at the Apache layer (server: Apache/2.4.37 RHEL). Data has been MOVED to /assets/*.json (see above).
endpoints.sample_response:
Forbidden

You don't have permission to access this resource.
endpoints.gradvisr_use: DO NOT USE. The 2022 uf_api cmapjson endpoints are permanently 403'd. Their data lives on at campusmap.ufl.edu/assets/<name>.json (bus_stops, campus_dining=dining, etc.) and the searchBldg API replaces geo_buildings. Record kept so the SDK never retries these.

endpoints.name: UF Events — LiveWhale Calendar JSON feed
endpoints.params: Optional LiveWhale filters (e.g. ?days=N, ?group=, ?category=, ?search=, ?max=). NOTE: UF calendar is LiveWhale, NOT Localist — the Localist /api/2/events path 404s here (confirmed: meta generator='LiveWhale Calendar'). events.ufl.edu does not resolve.
endpoints.returns: JSON array of event objects. Keys: id, gid, group_title, title, url, date, date_time, date_utc, date_iso, date_ts, tz_offset, timezone, date2_* (end), is_all_day, ... ~967KB for default window. Includes ISO8601 + unix ts for easy parsing.
endpoints.sample_response: [{"id":53965,"gid":4,"group_title":"UF Athletics","title":"Florida Gators Track and Field NCAA Outdoor East Preliminaries","url":"https://calendar.ufl.edu/event/53965-...","date":"May 30","date_utc":"2026-05-30 04:00:00","date_iso":"2026-05-30T00:00:00-04:00","date_ts":1780113600,"timezone":"America/New_York","is_all_day":1}]
endpoints.gradvisr_use: Surface UF events alongside the academic calendar (deadlines, athletics, group events) by group_title/date. NOT the registrar academic-deadline source (that's CourseLeaf/calendar of dates), but good for 'what's happening on campus this week' enrichment in the planner.

endpoints.name: UF Events — LiveWhale iCal feed
endpoints.params: None required (same LiveWhale filters supported). text/calendar.
endpoints.returns: Standard iCal VCALENDAR with VEVENT blocks (DTSTART/DTEND/LOCATION/SUMMARY/URL). ~807KB. PRODID=//calendar.ufl.edu//.
endpoints.gradvisr_use: Drop-in iCal subscription for users who want UF events in their own calendar app; or parse server-side for LOCATION-based event geocoding against searchBldg. RSS sibling at /live/rss/events also live (746KB, georss-tagged).

endpoints.name: UF Transit (Passio GO) — stops + routes
endpoints.params: POST form (Content-Type application/x-www-form-urlencoded). REQUIRED magic param: json={"s0":"3826","sA":1} (UF system id = 3826, username UniFlorida — discovered via getSystems). Then one of: getStops=2 (returns stops + embedded route metadata). NOTE: bare GET ?getStops=1&systemSelected0=3826 returns [{error no systemsAndRoutes}] — the json={...} form field is mandatory.
endpoints.returns: {stops:{ID<stopId>:{routeId, stopId, position, name, latitude, longitude, id, userId(=3826), radius, routeName, routeShortname, routeGroupId}}}. 67 stops. Routes derived from embedded routeId/routeName: 72448 Central, 72449 North-South, 72450 East-West.
endpoints.gradvisr_use: 67 geocoded UF shuttle stops on 3 named routes (Central/North-South/East-West). Combine with searchBldg lat/lon to answer 'which shuttle gets me from class A's building to class B's building' for tight back-to-back schedules — a stronger version of the walk-time feature.

endpoints.name: UF Transit (Passio GO) — live vehicle positions
endpoints.params: POST form: json={"s0":"3826","sA":1} + getBuses=1. Same json={...} requirement.
endpoints.returns: {buses:{<routeId>:[{busName, route, routeName, latitude, longitude, speed, calculatedCourse, paxLoad, outOfService, ...}]}, excludedRoutes:[...], time:{"3826":"HH:MM AM"}, debug:{systemIds:[3826]}}. Structure verified; returned ZERO active vehicles this run (excludedRoutes:[-1,72448,72449,72450], time 07:49 AM late-May = summer break / no service) — the empty result is correct off-service behavior, not a dead endpoint.
endpoints.gradvisr_use: Real-time bus AVL. Low planner value (only useful day-of, and empty during breaks/summer). Keep for a possible 'next shuttle' widget during fall/spring. Re-verify vehicle payload shape during an active service term.

endpoints.name: Passio GO system directory (getSystems)
endpoints.params: getSystems=1 (GET works here, no json param). Used once to discover UF's system id.
endpoints.returns: JSON object keyed by index -> {fullname, username, goAgencyName, id, goColor, logo, ...} for every Passio agency (~98KB). UF = {id:'3826', fullname:'University of Florida', username:'UniFlorida'}.
endpoints.gradvisr_use: Discovery only — confirms UF system id 3826. Hardcode 3826 in the SDK; no need to re-query. Also gives FIU(4119), FGCU(2281) ids if Gradvisr expands to other FL schools.

endpoints.name: RecSports gym occupancy (Connect2Concepts / goboardapi)
endpoints.params: AccountAPIKey=8e2c21d2-6f5d-45c1-af9e-c23aebfda68b (UF RecSports public key, extracted live from recsports.ufl.edu/cameras-counts/ widget serviceUrl). This is the JSON backend behind the connect2mycloud.com/Widgets/Data/locationCount?type=circle&key=... iframe widget.
endpoints.returns: JSON array of 22 facility-area records. Keys: LocationName, FacilityName, LastCount, TotalCapacity, IsClosed, LastUpdatedDateAndTime (+ more). Live counts e.g. SWRC Weight Room 97/300, SWRC Cardio Room 2 = 3/75.
endpoints.gradvisr_use: Live real-time gym/pool crowding (% capacity) for SWRC, Southwest, Lake Wauburg, etc. Nice student-life enrichment widget — 'best time to hit the gym around your class schedule'. 22 distinct areas. Re-derive the AccountAPIKey from the cameras-counts page if UF rotates it.

endpoints.name: RecSports live gym cameras
endpoints.params: cam1.jpg .. cam8.jpg (8 cameras). Optional cache-bust ?t=<epoch_ms>. Browser UA + Referer https://recsports.ufl.edu/cameras-counts/ fine.
endpoints.returns: Live still-frame image/jpeg snapshots of gym floors (verified live: cam1 326KB, cam2 318KB, cam8 431KB, all HTTP 200 image/jpeg). Refresh the URL to get a new frame.
endpoints.sample_response: (binary JPEG, image/jpeg, cam1.jpg=326014 bytes, cam8.jpg=431036 bytes)
endpoints.gradvisr_use: Live gym webcams (8 feeds). Pure student-life flavor — pair with the occupancy JSON for a 'see how busy the gym is right now' card. Not planner-relevant; low priority but confirmed still working per the brief's ask.

surfaces.surface: Campus + Events + Transit (campusmap.ufl.edu, stars.facilities.ufl.edu, calendar.ufl.edu LiveWhale, Passio GO transit, recsports.ufl.edu + Connect2Concepts)
surfaces.summary: Re-attacked the locked 2022 cmapjson endpoints and the rest of the campus/events/transit surface, all verified live this run. KEY CORRECTIONS to the 2022 uf_api picture: (1) The /library/cmapjson/*.json static files (dining/study/bus_stops/geo_buildings/aed) are PERMANENTLY DEAD — hard Apache 403 even with realistic browser UA + Referer (deny-all on the directory, not a WAF). (2) campusmap.ufl.edu is now an Angular SPA whose bundle revealed entirely new live backends. The data MOVED to two places: a building/room directory API at /library/api/searchBldg (4149 rows, 2789 buildings, each with LAT/LON — the gold for time-between-classes), and 60+ GeoJSON layer files at /assets/<name>.json (bus_stops, campus_dining, libraries, rec_fitness, computer_labs, etc.), self-described by /assets/sidenav.json. Plus public building photos at stars.facilities.ufl.edu/public/api/photos/photo?bldg=. EVENTS: UF is NOT on Localist — it's LiveWhale Calendar; the Localist /api/2/events path 404s. Real feeds are calendar.ufl.edu/live/json/events (+ /live/ical + /live/rss), all live with full ISO/unix timestamps. TRANSIT: UF runs Passio GO, system id 3826 (username UniFlorida, found via getSystems). The working call is POST passio3.com/www/mapGetData.php with form field json={"s0":"3826","sA":1} + getStops=2 (67 geocoded stops, 3 routes: Central/North-South/East-West) or getBuses=1 (live vehicles; empty right now = correct summer-break off-service behavior). RECSPORTS: 8 gym cams live (cam1-8.jpg) and live occupancy JSON via goboardapi.azurewebsites.net FacilityCount with the public AccountAPIKey extracted from the cameras-counts widget (22 areas, real-time counts like SWRC Weight Room 97/300). Relevance is mostly enrichment/heritage except searchBldg, which is genuinely core (geocodes every class building for walk/shuttle-time-between-classes).
surfaces.host: campusmap.ufl.edu (+ stars.facilities.ufl.edu, calendar.ufl.edu, passio3.com, recsports.ufl.edu, goboardapi.azurewebsites.net)
surfaces.how_to_use: CORE recipe (time-between-classes): GET https://campusmap.ufl.edu/library/api/searchBldg?q=x with a desktop browser User-Agent and Referer https://campusmap.ufl.edu/ -> get full array of {BLDG, NAME, ABBREV, LAT, LON}; build a {ABBREV/BLDG -> (LAT,LON)} lookup. For each pair of consecutive sections in a student's schedule (building from ONE.UF soc), haversine the two LAT/LONs, divide by ~1.4 m/s walk speed, flag tight transitions. Optionally route via shuttle: POST https://passio3.com/www/mapGetData.php form-encoded with json={"s0":"3826","sA":1}&getStops=2 -> nearest stop to each building by lat/lon, same route -> shuttle option. ENRICHMENT layers: GET https://campusmap.ufl.edu/assets/sidenav.json once to enumerate layer filenames, then lazy GET https://campusmap.ufl.edu/assets/<filename>.json (GeoJSON) for libraries/dining/printers/rec near class buildings. Building photo: GET https://stars.facilities.ufl.edu/public/api/photos/photo?bldg=<4digit BLDG> -> JPEG. EVENTS: GET https://calendar.ufl.edu/live/json/events -> array with title/url/group_title/date_iso/date_ts. GYM: GET https://goboardapi.azurewebsites.net/api/FacilityCount/GetCountsByAccount?AccountAPIKey=8e2c21d2-6f5d-45c1-af9e-c23aebfda68b -> live % capacity per area; cams at https://recsports.ufl.edu/cam/cam{1..8}.jpg. All endpoints are public/unauthenticated; send one realistic browser UA. Hardcode UF transit system id 3826 and the RecSports AccountAPIKey (both stable, public). Re-derive the AccountAPIKey from recsports.ufl.edu/cameras-counts/ if it ever rotates.
new_discoveries: campusmap.ufl.edu/library/api/searchBldg — undocumented building+room directory API, 4149 rows / 2789 buildings, each geocoded LAT/LON. Never in the 2022 uf_api repo. This is the single most useful thing found: it powers time/walk between back-to-back classes.

The 2022 cmapjson/*.json files are not just blocked — the data RELOCATED to campusmap.ufl.edu/assets/*.json (60+ GeoJSON layers) under the new Angular SPA. Found by fetching main-es2015.js and grepping for asset/api paths.
campusmap.ufl.edu/assets/sidenav.json is a self-describing manifest listing every layer's FILENAME and category — a discovery index for the whole campus layer set.
stars.facilities.ufl.edu/public/api/photos/photo?bldg=<code> returns a real public JPEG photo of any building keyed by the same BLDG code as searchBldg.
UF events are on LiveWhale Calendar, NOT Localist (the brief's assumption). Localist /api/2/* 404s; the real feeds are calendar.ufl.edu/live/json|ical|rss/events. events.ufl.edu does not resolve.

UF transit AVL is Passio GO, system id 3826 / username 'UniFlorida' (found via passio3.com getSystems). The non-obvious working signature is POST mapGetData.php with a json={...} form field — bare query params return 'error no systemsAndRoutes'. getStops=2 yields 67 stops on routes 72448 Central / 72449 North-South / 72450 East-West.

Live UF gym occupancy JSON: goboardapi.azurewebsites.net/api/FacilityCount/GetCountsByAccount with public AccountAPIKey 8e2c21d2-6f5d-45c1-af9e-c23aebfda68b (extracted from the cameras-counts widget's serviceUrl). 22 areas with real-time LastCount/TotalCapacity/IsClosed.
RecSports gym cams still live: recsports.ufl.edu/cam/cam1.jpg .. cam8.jpg (8 feeds, ~300-430KB JPEGs).
recommendations: Make searchBldg a first-class SDK module (UFCampus.buildings()) — cache the 2789-building geocode table; it is the backbone of the time-between-classes feature and is the only CORE-relevance find here.

Add a UFCampus.walkTime(buildingA, buildingB) helper: haversine on searchBldg lat/lon, ~1.4 m/s, with a shuttle-alternative path computed from the Passio getStops feed (67 stops, 3 routes). Hardcode system id 3826.
Replace any code that assumes UF Events = Localist. Wire the events module to calendar.ufl.edu/live/json/events (LiveWhale). Note Localist /api/2/* will 404 forever here.

Drive all campusmap.ufl.edu requests with a desktop browser User-Agent + Referer https://campusmap.ufl.edu/ — without UA you risk the same 403 family. The /library/cmapjson/ legacy directory is dead; never retry it.

Treat the gym occupancy/cams and live bus positions as low-priority enrichment widgets, not planner core. Re-verify the Passio getBuses vehicle payload shape and the goboard counts during an active fall/spring term (this run hit summer off-service, so buses were empty by design).
Pull /assets/sidenav.json at build time to auto-generate the list of available campus layers, so the SDK self-updates if UF adds/renames layers.
Store the RecSports AccountAPIKey and Passio system id 3826 as documented public constants with a note on how to re-derive them (cameras-counts page / getSystems) in case UF rotates them.

surfaces.surface: ONE.UF SOC deep (apix/soc) — definitive query reference + satellite services
surfaces.summary: Exhausted the ONE.UF Schedule-of-Courses surface live (2026-05-30, term Spring 2026 = 2261). There are exactly TWO data-bearing SOC endpoints — /apix/soc/filters (dictionaries) and /apix/soc/schedule (the course/section list) — both public, unauthenticated, GET, JSON, returning 200 this run. Every other /apix/soc/* subpath (courses, section, syllabus, prereqs, etc.) is 404. The canonical query-param vocabulary was extracted directly from the SOC SPA bundle (one.uf.edu/soc/soc.59d5c440492d70a0ad41.min.js) and then each param was tested live against a baseline of TOTALROWS=4622 to prove which actually filter in 2026. KEY CORRECTION vs the 2022 uf_api repo and common assumptions: boolean flags (gen-ed, writing, online, day-of-week, eep, elal, ai, auf) filter ONLY with value `=true` — `=on`/`=1` are ignored and silently return the full set. GE param names are ge-b/ge-c/ge-h/ge-m/ge-n/ge-p/ge-s (NOT ge=B); ge-d exists in the label map but returns 0. Writing flags are wr-2000/wr-4000/wr-6000 (NOT writing-2/4/6). Quest flags are qst-1..qst-4. Credits filter via credits=<n> + cred-srch=EQ|LE|GE (uppercase). Pagination is cursor-style: page size 50, feed the response's LASTCONTROLNUMBER back as last-control-number. Section records carry 35 fields incl. simpleSyllabusParams ('Spring 2026/EEL/3135/11037' = term/subject/catalog/classNumber), meetTimes, waitList, dropaddDeadline, finalExam, genEd[], quest[], grWriting, deptCode/deptName, prerequisites (course-level free text). SATELLITES: /apix/response-cache/soc (+/filters +/schedule) return HTTP 200 with Content-Length:0 and only set an ONEUF_SESSION cookie — pure server-side cache-warm/session-prime, no body, no usable data. The SimpleSyllabus service (uf.simplesyllabus.com tenant EXISTS) is NOT publicly reachable by the param string — every doc path falls through to /static/notfound.html and cross-origin fetch is CORS-blocked; the syllabus PDF sits behind UF SSO/tenant session. NEW DISCOVERY: an authenticated alternate of the schedule endpoint exists — /api/myschedule/course-search — the SPA swaps to it when logged in (out of scope, auth-walled, do not use). filters dictionary in 2026: 203 departments (8-digit code -> name), 49 terms, 8 prog-levels, 3 categories.
surfaces.how_to_use: CANONICAL SCHEDULE REQUEST: GET https://one.uf.edu/apix/soc/schedule?category=CWSP&term=<TERMCODE>&last-control-number=0  (no auth, no headers needed beyond a normal UA). Required: category + term + last-control-number. category values: CWSP (Campus/Web/Special — the default, use this), UFOL (UF Online), IA (Innovation Academy). TERMCODE from /apix/soc/filters terms[].CODE, e.g. 2261=Spring2026, 2268=Fall2026, 2265=Summer2026 (sub-terms: 22656W1=SummerA, 22656W2=SummerB, 22651=SummerC). PATTERN of the term code: 2 + last2-of-year + {1=Spring,5=Summer,8=Fall}. PAGINATION: response is an array with one object {COURSES:[], LASTCONTROLNUMBER, RETRIEVEDROWS, TOTALROWS}. Page size = 50. To page: take LASTCONTROLNUMBER from the response and pass it as &last-control-number=<that value>; stop when RETRIEVEDROWS<50 or you have TOTALROWS. WORKING FILTERS (verified live, all AND-combine): dept=<8-digit code from filters> (e.g. 19050000=ECE -> 74 rows); prog-level=UGRD|GRAD|LAW|MED|PHM|PA|PROF|VEM; course-code=<prefix or full, e.g. EEL or EEL3135>; class-num=<5-digit class number>; course-title=<text>; instructor=<lastname>; level-min=<3000>&level-max=<3999> (catalog-level band); credits=<n>&cred-srch=EQ|LE|GE; gen-ed: ge-b|ge-c|ge-h|ge-m|ge-n|ge-p|ge-s=true (Biological/Composition/Humanities/Math/Physical/IntlSocietal-Social? — labels: ge-h=Humanities, ge-m=Math, ge-n=Physical/Natural, ge-p, ge-s=Social&Behavioral); writing: wr-2000|wr-4000|wr-6000=true; quest: qst-1|qst-2|qst-3|qst-4=true; online delivery: online-c=true (Primarily Classroom — huge), online-p (Primarily online), online-a (All online?), online-h (Hybrid); day-of-week: day-m|day-t|day-w|day-r|day-f|day-s=true; eep=true (EEP eligible), elal=true (Experiential Learning), ai=true (additional-instruction), auf=true. CRITICAL: send boolean flags as =true (NOT =on, NOT =1 — those are ignored and return everything). FILTERS DICTIONARY: GET https://one.uf.edu/apix/soc/filters -> {categories[], progLevels[], terms[], departments[]}; each dept is {CODE:'19050000', DESC:'Electrical and Computer Engineering'} — use this as the dept-code->name map (203 entries) and to drive a full-catalog crawl (loop terms x depts, paginate each). SYLLABUS: read section.simpleSyllabusParams ('Spring 2026/EEL/3135/11037') as structured data (term/subject/catalog/classNumber) — do NOT expect to fetch the actual syllabus doc; uf.simplesyllabus.com is SSO-gated. SKIP /apix/response-cache/* (empty bodies) and /api/myschedule/* (auth-walled).
